rulings.Case Title: CFO RICK, INC VS. KRAEMER, NICK, AKA DOORMAN PROPERTY MANAGEMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS KRAEMER, NICK, AKA DOORMAN PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, RAFTERY, BARRETT, AKA DOORMAN PROPERTY MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendants and cross-complainants Nick Kraemer, Barrett Raftery and Doorman Property Management S Corp.'s motion for summary judgment or alternatively motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. There are triable disputes whether: 1) plaintiff and cross-defendant CFO Rick, Inc. performed all of the duties required of it per the parties' contract or was excused from doing so; 2) any of the services performed or required to be performed by CFO Rick per the parties' contract required CFO Rick, Rick Belgrade or the persons who performed those services to be a licensed CPA; and 3) CFO Rick or Mr. Belgrade made any misrepresentation in connection with their statements that they performed accounting services. Mr. Belgrade's declaration alone creates triable disputes on all of these issues. Apparently recognizing that, the reply repeatedly states that the declaration is "not credible" and should be disregarded, citing Estate of Housley (1997) 56 Cal. App. 4th 342, 259-60.) Housley is not helpful to defendants. That case states that "the sole declaration of a party opposing a summary judgment motion which raise a triable issue of fact is sufficient to deny that motion¿.Code of Civil Procedure section 437c, subdivision (e) does not allow a trial court to weigh the credibility of a declaration submitted by the party opposing a summary judgment motion and then grant the motion, except where the declaration is facially so incredible as a matter of law that the moving party otherwise would be entitled to summary judgment." Because the face of Mr. Belgrade's declaration does not disclose anything incredible about any of the statements in that declaration, the extremely narrow and very likely almost never successfully invoked "facial incredible" exception mentioned but found inapplicable in Housley is also inapplicable here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CFO Rick is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

Kendrick], 2. Statement Of Compliance And Production Of Records In Response To The Fourth Request For Production Of Documents [Rfd #4] [Stephen H. Kendrick], 3. Statement Of Compliance And Production Of Records In Response To Fitfh Request For Production Of Documents [Rfd #5] [Stephen H. Kendrick], 4. Monetary Sanctions Directed At Stephen H. Kendrick For The Failure And Refusal To Pro Tem Judge Chuck Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar per request of the moving party. (email request 5/18/18) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to cgeerhart@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAGDALENO MERAZ ET AL VS. PLANT CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT DEGENKOLB ENGINEERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT . Defendant Degenkolb Engineers' motion for summary judgment is denied. There are triable disputes whether Degenkolb: 1) owed a duty to plaintiff Magdaleno Merz; 2) breached a duty of care to Mr. Merz; 3) is a legal cause of Mr. Merz's claimed injuries. (Scott Depo. 9:23-10:3, 32:19-34:4; Nelson Depo. 10:11-11:13, 38:4-19; Glabe Dec. pars. 10-11, 15-16, and 27 ). Degenkolb's objections to the declaration of David Glabe are overruled. rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, MARTIN MCNERNEY DEVELOPMENT, INC.,, 418 JESSIE HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC,, 418 JESSIE PROPERTIES, LLC.'S, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Martin McNerney Development, Inc., Fifth Historic Properties, LLC, 418 Jessie Historic Properties, LLC and 418 Jessie Properties, LLC's demurrer to the first, third, sixth and eighth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Mint Collection-410-418 Jessie Street Condominium Owners' Association is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third and sixth causes of action for violation of residential building standards , strict liability and breach of fiduciary duty; and b) sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the eighth cause of action for unlawful LLC distributions. The first cause of action fails because the residential standards only apply to new or original construction and the allegations in the FAC make clear that the construction involved in this case is neither new nor original. The third cause of action fails because strict liability only applies to mass-produced homes and the construction involved in this case did not involve mass-produced homes. The sixth cause of action fails because there are no facts alleged in the FAC, nor is reasonably possible that there could be any alleged facts, supporting a fiduciary duty owed to the Association by any of the demurring defendants. The eighth cause fails because there are no facts alleged showing any unlawful distribution, but leave to amend is granted because it is reasonably possible that the Association may be able to allege such facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: LOURDES ZAMUDIO ET AL VS. CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Against Lourdes Zamudio
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT CARDIOVASCULAR RESEARCH FOUNDATION'S, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Cardiovascular Research Foundation's motion for summary judgment on the sole cause of action for breach of contract in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Lourdes Zamudio is granted. Assuming without deciding that the indemnity provision of the 2007 CRF-Marriott contract provides for a first party claim by a Marriott employee, the undisputed facts establish that the indemnity provision does not impose liability on CRF for Alexander Damhuis' intentional torts. The phrase "indemnifying party's ¿ willful misconduct" in the indemnity provision is not reasonably susceptible to Ms. Zamudio's proffered interpretation that it covers all willful misconduct committed by a CRF employee regardless of whether that misconduct had any connection to the employee's employment. Ms. Zamudio's extrinsic evidence does not create a triable dispute regarding her interpretation of the indemnity provision because her interpretation is at odds with the language of that provision and there is no evidence that the parties mistakenly misused that language or intended other language. The willful misconduct that is covered by the indemnity provision is limited to the willful misconduct committed by CRF itself, which, by operation of well-established principles of vicarious liability, only includes willful misconduct of an employee within the course and scope of the employee's employment. CRF is not vicariously liable for Mr. Damhuis' intentional torts as a matter of law because there is no causal nexus between Mr. Damhuis' tortious conduct and his work as a CRF's employee. (Lisa M. v. Henry Mayo Newhall Memorial Hospital (1995) 12 Cal.4th 291.) The undisputed facts show that Mr. Damhuis and Ms. Zamudio had never met before the incident, Mr. Damhuis' role as CRF's education coordinator did not include interaction with hotel employees, and Mr. Hartman never asked Mr. Damhuis to enter Mr. Hartman's room. The fact that Mr. Damhuis was in the hotel, and therefore able to commit the tort, because he was a CRF employee is insufficient to establish the required causal nexus. Mr. Damhuis' intentional sexual assault is not fairly attributed to any aspect of Mr. Damhuis' employment with CRF. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CRF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA COHEN VS. UNUM GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Unum Group, Provident Life And Accident Insurance Company'S Motion For Leave To Take Deposition Of City Of Oakland Representative.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 8, Defendants Unum Group, Provident Life And Accident Insurance Company's Motion For Leave To Take Deposition Of City Of Oakland Representative. Defendants Unum Group and Provident Life and Accident Insurance Company's application for leave to take the deposition of City of Oakland Representative after discovery cutoff deadline is granted. Defendants may take the depositions of all three City of Oakland representatives on the first date all three are available and counsel for both sides are available. Defendants have shown good cause for taking the depositions after the discovery cutoff due to the unavailability of two of the three witnesses and the efficiencies of taking all three depositions on the same day. While defendants might have been more diligent in seeking the depositions of the City of Oakland repesentatives, any lack of diligence is not a ground to deny it potentially highly probative discovery such as the depositions of the City of Oakland representatives. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Provident is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA COHEN VS. UNUM GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANTS UNUM GROUP, PROVIDENT LIFE AND ACCIDENT INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants Unum Group and Provident Life and Accident Insurance Company's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff Barbara Cohen's second cause of action for breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing and request for punitive damages is denied as to both issues. There are triable disputes whether defendants' denial of policy benefits to Ms. Cohen was: 1) unreasonable and 2) done in conscious and willful disregard of Ms. Cohen's rights under the policy that subjected Ms. Cohen to cruel and unjust hardship and was despicable. Ms. Cohen's ability to successfully carry out her duties as a hearing officer does not negate the possibility that defendants' denial of policy benefits to her was unreasonable and/or taken in a manner that would allow a reasonable jury to find that defendants acted with malice and/or oppression within the meaning of Civil Code 3294(c)(1) and (2).Viewed in the light most favorable to Ms. Cohen, the evidence submitted by Ms. Cohen could support a determination by a reasonable jury that defendants knowingly, without regard to the effect it would have on Ms. Cohen and to save money chose to disregard the medically and vocationally well-founded views of Ms. Cohen and her health care professionals and their own vocational expert to assert without any factual support that Ms. Cohen was able to perform the material duties of a trial attorney and they did so without considering recent medical information that they knew existed but did not have. Defendants' objections to paragraph 26 of the declaration of Ryan Opgenorth and exhibits 1-7 of that declaration and exhibits 1-28 to Ms. Cohen's request for judicial notice are sustained. All other objections to evidence made by defendants are overruled. Ms. Cohen's objections to the evidence submitted with the reply papers are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Cohen is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) by citing only those facts in her statement of additional facts that relate to defendants' denial of Ms. Cohen's policy benefits and excludes facts pertaining to asserted past misconduct of defendants not involving Ms. Cohen and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA COHEN VS. UNUM GROUP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Unum Group, Provident Life And Accident Insurance Company'S Motion To Compel Physical Exam Of Plaintiff.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 8, Defendants Unum Group, Provident Life And Accident Insurance Company's Motion To Compel Physical Exam Of Plaintiff. Defendant Provident Life and Accident Insurance Company's motion to compel physical examination of plaintiff Barbara Cohen is granted. There is good cause for a physical examination of Ms. Cohen and, if it is unable to be scheduled prior to the close of the discovery cutoff date, there is also good cause for the examination to occur after the discovery cutoff date. While Provident might have been more diligent in seeking an examination of Ms. Cohen, any lack of diligence by Provident is not a ground to deny it potentially highly probative discovery such as the exmination of Ms. Cohen. If the parties are unable to agree on the scheduling and details of that examination after good faith meet and confer efforts, they should seek resolution of their disputes by ex parte application. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Provident is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SOPHOM PREAP VS. DILIP MISRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 24, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANTS DILIP MISRA, MONICA GANDHI'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants Dilip Misra and Monica Gandhi's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Sophom Preap is denied. Ms. Preap adequately alleges facts showing malice for purposes of punitive damages liability. The allegations that Mr. Misra intentionally attempted to flee the scene of the accident knowing that he had hit and injured Ms. Preap which caused Ms. Preap to suffer additional fear, distress and anxiety are sufficient to show despicable conduct carried on with willful and conscious disregard for Ms. Preap's rights. Defendants' reliance on Broooks v. E.J. Wilig Truck Transp. Co. (1953) 40 Cal. 2d 669 is misplaced. Nothing in that case precludes punitive damages liability based on the previously mentioned allegations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Preap is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
